High-level design interview prep HLD
Architecture, distributed systems trade-offs, and end-to-end system walkthroughs for classic system design rounds.
Low-level design interview prep LLD
Object modeling, extensible APIs, machine-coding structure, and follow-up handling for class-design rounds.
Data structures and algorithms interview prep DSA
Curated coding-round practice across general problem sets and company-specific question banks for array, tree, graph, DP, and mock-round prep.

End-to-end AI/ML engineering from foundations through LLMs, RAG, agents, MLOps, and responsible AI for building production intelligent systems.

Curriculum → AI Engineer learning path → Prompt engineering and RAG → Building with LLM frameworks

Lesson 3 of 3

Building with LLM frameworks

LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack, and other orchestration frameworks for composing LLM-powered applications.

25-35 min AI/ML flow Prompt engineering and RAG 4 guided stops

Study map

What this lesson helps you explain

Frameworks abstract common patterns (chains, retrieval, memory) and accelerate development of production LLM applications.

Start here Frame the trade-off before diving into architecture. 1. Framework landscape LangChain provides composable chains, agents, and tool integrations2. Core abstractions Chains compose multiple LLM calls and transformations into workflows3. When to use vs build custom Start with a framework for rapid prototyping and validation

Guided walkthrough

Move through the lesson in a clear interview order

4 steps

Start with the framing, walk the concept in order, then use the right rail for prompts, pitfalls, and visual anchors.

1. Interview framing Frameworks abstract common patterns (chains, retrieval, memory) and accelerate development of production LLM applications. 2. Framework landscape LangChain provides composable chains, agents, and tool integrations3. Core abstractions Chains compose multiple LLM calls and transformations into workflows4. When to use vs build custom Start with a framework for rapid prototyping and validation
1

Start with this framing

Interview framing

Frameworks abstract common patterns (chains, retrieval, memory) and accelerate development of production LLM applications.

What to say first

  • Can choose an appropriate framework for the project requirements.
  • Understands framework abstractions well enough to debug and extend them.
  • Knows when custom code is preferable to framework abstractions.
2

Study section

Framework landscape

LangChain, LlamaIndex, Haystack, and Semantic Kernel each emphasize different aspects of LLM application development.

Call these points out explicitly

  • LangChain provides composable chains, agents, and tool integrations
  • LlamaIndex focuses on data indexing and retrieval optimization
  • Haystack emphasizes production pipelines with modular components
3

Study section

Core abstractions

Chains, retrievers, memory, output parsers, and callbacks provide reusable building blocks for complex applications.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Chains compose multiple LLM calls and transformations into workflows
  • Memory modules maintain conversation context across turns
  • Output parsers enforce structured responses and handle parsing failures
4

Study section

When to use vs build custom

Frameworks accelerate prototyping but may introduce overhead, abstraction leaks, or vendor lock-in for production systems.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Start with a framework for rapid prototyping and validation
  • Consider custom implementations when you need full control over latency and cost
  • Evaluate framework maturity, community support, and maintenance trajectory

Checklist

Things to say clearly

  • Can choose an appropriate framework for the project requirements.
  • Understands framework abstractions well enough to debug and extend them.
  • Knows when custom code is preferable to framework abstractions.

Common pitfalls

What to avoid

  • Over-engineering with frameworks when a simple API call would suffice.
  • Coupling business logic tightly to a specific framework version.
  • Not profiling framework overhead in latency-sensitive applications.

Interview prompts

Practice aloud

  • Compare LangChain and LlamaIndex for a document Q&A use case.
  • When would you build a custom RAG pipeline instead of using a framework?
  • How do you handle framework version upgrades in production applications?

Related topics

Keep the thread going

Interactive practice lab

Write, save, and move through the mock interview

Answers stay in your browser so you can come back later.

0/3 saved Step 1/3: Explain the concept
Ln 1, Col 1 1 lines 0 words 0.0 KB markdown

PRACTICE GUIDE

Explain the concept Demonstrate deep understanding by explaining the topic clearly, covering intuition, algorithms, and practical applications.

Explain the concept

Explain "Building with LLM frameworks" as if teaching a senior engineer who is new to AI/ML. Cover the core intuition, when and why you would use it, and the key algorithms or techniques involved.

Goal

Demonstrate deep understanding by explaining the topic clearly, covering intuition, algorithms, and practical applications.

Guardrails

  • Compare LangChain and LlamaIndex for a document Q&A use case.
  • Framework landscape
  • Core abstractions
  • Can choose an appropriate framework for the project requirements.

Suggested Structure

  • Core intuition
  • Key algorithms or techniques
  • When to apply
  • Common variants
